> signal-coach-core@0.0.0 prebuild
> npm run check:rps-ux-guardrails


> signal-coach-core@0.0.0 check:rps-ux-guardrails
> node scripts/check-rps-ux-guardrails.mjs

RPS UX guardrail check passed.

> signal-coach-core@0.0.0 build
> vite build

startBody.hcpReply: I need something more specific before I can react to that.

realism.rewrittenLine: I need something more specific before I can react to that.

runtime.respondReply: I can listen, but this needs to be specific for my practice.

scenarios.placeholder: Open the conversation. Read the scene and cues carefully.

Open the conversation. Read the scene and cues carefully.

checks.firstHcpLine: I can listen, but this needs to be specific for my practice.
checks.note: Initial-access opener should be busy but professional, not rude or flattened.

checks.firstHcpLine: I can listen, but this needs to be specific for my practice. But I need to understand how this changes what I'm already doing. If this is about the main issue here, that's still been a real sticking point for us.
checks.note: Follow-up framing should not reset to proxy or first-access language.

checks.firstHcpLine: I can listen, but this needs to be specific for my practice. But I need to understand how this changes what I'm already doing. Yes, figuring out the right patient is still one of the harder parts here.
checks.note: Skeptical objections should sound grounded, not templated or theatrically rude.

checks.firstHcpLine: I can listen, but this needs to be specific for my practice. Yes, that's where we keep running into trouble. But I need to understand how this changes what I'm already doing.
checks.note: Access/formulary responses should be complete, process-aware clinician language.
